title: Print Squares

content.problemStatement:
Write a program that utilizes a while loop to print the squares of numbers from 1 to NNN.

Check the sample input / output below further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the squares of the integers from 1 to N inclusive, separated by a single space. Print each test case's result on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case with N = 5. The squares of 1 through 5 are printed.
Single test case, N = 10. Squares from 1^2 up to 10^2 are output.
There is a single test case with N = 2. The squares of 1 and 2 are printed.

solution.approach: The problem only requires generating the square of each integer from 1 to N. A simple linear scan using a while‑loop (or for‑loop) suffices. For each i from 1 to N we compute i*i and append it to the output. Because N ≤ 1000, the total work per test case is O(N) and the overall complexity is O(∑N) which easily fits the limits. No extra data structures are needed, only a StringBuilder for fast concatenation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(∑N) overall
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0‑based)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 1
        print(n)
    elif i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 2
        print(n)
    elif i == 2:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 3
        print(n)
    elif i == 3:
        t = 2
        print(t)
        print(1)
        print(1)
    elif i == 4:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(1000)
    elif i == 5:
        t = 2
        print(t)
        print(1000)
        print(1000)
    elif i == 6:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999)
    elif i == 7:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(1000)
        print(999)
        print(998)
    elif i == 8:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for _ in range(5):
            print(5)
    elif i == 9:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        for _ in range(3):
            print(3)
    elif i == 10:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        for n in [1,2,3,4]:
            print(n)
    elif i == 11:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        for n in [4,3,2,1]:
            print(n)
    elif i == 12:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999)
    elif i == 13:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(500)
    elif i == 14:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(123)
    elif i == 15:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(321)
    elif i == 16:
        t = 10
        print(t)
        for n in range(1,11):
            print(n)
    elif i == 17:
        t = 20
        print(t)
        for _ in range(20):
            print(1000)
    elif i == 18:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for n in [10,20,30,40,50]:
            print(n)
    elif i == 19:
        t = 6
        print(t)
        for n in [15,25,35,45,55,65]:
            print(n)
